ref: You ordered us to bring him here, so you could see him for yourself.
text: An ka kaŋɛ fo loŋ na nɛ fo kaŋɛ anyi, fo anya le: ‘Men ya bar mo nɛ ma alɛ gba e wu mo.’
translated: He said, "If you see a woman and she is beautiful, tell her, 'I love you, and I want to marry you.'"

ref: When Judah came along, he did not recognize her because of the veil. He thought she was a prostitute
text: Juda ka ba fo kakpa nɛ Taama tase na nseŋ wu mo nɛ e fɛ fanɛ sakaleche ko e la mo ŋkpal e ka ta shata m buu anishi so so.
translated: Juda went to the farm and Taama followed him. They worked together until evening and then they returned home tired.

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Kusɔ nɛ k daga fanɛ fo wora e la fanɛ fo yɔ fo nananyɛn Bɛtuwɛl pe ashi Mɛsopoteemia epul ere to n ya fin fo wɔpa Leeban be mbichebiana na be eko n ta ŋ ki fo eche.
translated: The Gonja people migrated from a place called Bɛtuwɛl in Mesopotamia and settled in Lebanon before moving to their current location.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Epeŋi daŋ pete nna pɔɛŋ nɛ Lɔt fo Zowar.
translated: The chief's son went to Lot's farm in Zowar.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Juda ka ya ta kaboefɔlbi na n shuŋi mo teri Haira nɛ e shi Adulam na fanɛ e ta n ya sa kache na nseŋ sɔ mbe asɔ na m ba, nɛ kache na maŋ naa wɔ kakpa nɛ b daŋ tu mo na.
translated: Juda went to the wilderness and stayed there for a long time. He then went to Haira and stayed there for a while. He then went to Adulam and stayed there for a long time. He then went to the land of the Philistines and stayed there for a long time. He then went back to his own land.
